parentQuestion: Epithelial tissues have all of the following general characteristics except they...

options: Tissue that covers body surfaces and lines cavities
scaffolds.explain: EPITHELIAL TISSUE COVERS body surfaces and LINES body cavities and organs. It forms the skin's outer layer and the inner lining of digestive, respiratory, and urinary tracts.

wrongExplains.0: Fat storage is adipose tissue (connective tissue).
wrongExplains.3: Signal transmission is nervous tissue's function.

options: Material between cells that provides support
scaffolds.explain: The EXTRACELLULAR MATRIX (ECM) is material BETWEEN CELLS that provides structural support and biochemical signals. Connective tissue has abundant ECM; epithelium has minimal ECM.

wrongExplains.0: Material inside cells is cytoplasm; ECM is outside cells.
wrongExplains.2: The cell membrane surrounds individual cells; ECM fills spaces between cells.
wrongExplains.3: The nucleus contains DNA; ECM is extracellular material.

scaffolds.q: How are epithelial cells arranged compared to connective tissue cells?
options: Scattered with lots of ECM between them
scaffolds.explain: Epithelial cells are TIGHTLY PACKED with MINIMAL ECM between them. This is the opposite of connective tissue, where cells are scattered through abundant ECM.

wrongExplains.0: Scattered arrangement with lots of ECM describes connective tissue.
wrongExplains.2: Epithelial cells form organized sheets, not random clusters.
wrongExplains.3: Long fibers characterize connective tissue, not epithelium.

scaffolds.q: Which tissue type has cells separated by large amounts of extracellular matrix?
scaffolds.explain: CONNECTIVE TISSUE has cells separated by LARGE AMOUNTS of EXTRACELLULAR MATRIX. This includes bone, cartilage, blood, and loose connective tissue.

wrongExplains.0: Epithelial cells are tightly packed with minimal ECM.
wrongExplains.2: Muscle tissue cells are packed together with minimal ECM.
wrongExplains.3: Nervous tissue has neurons and supporting cells, not abundant ECM.

options: A thin layer that anchors epithelium to underlying tissue
The thickest part of connective tissue
scaffolds.explain: The BASEMENT MEMBRANE is a thin layer of ECM that ANCHORS EPITHELIUM to underlying connective tissue. All epithelial tissues rest on a basement membrane.

wrongExplains.0: The cell's outer covering is the plasma membrane, not basement membrane.
wrongExplains.2: The basement membrane is thin, not thick.
wrongExplains.3: Organelle membranes are inside cells; basement membrane is extracellular.

options: Only the top layer is vascular
scaffolds.explain: Epithelium is AVASCULAR — it LACKS BLOOD VESSELS. Nutrients must diffuse from blood vessels in the underlying connective tissue through the basement membrane.

wrongExplains.0: Epithelium lacks blood vessels; it's avascular.
wrongExplains.2: No layer of epithelium contains blood vessels.
wrongExplains.3: All epithelial types are avascular without exception.

scaffolds.q: What does polarity mean in epithelium?
options: Cells have distinct apical (top) and basal (bottom) surfaces
scaffolds.explain: Epithelial POLARITY means cells have distinct APICAL (top, exposed) and BASAL (bottom, attached) surfaces. Each surface has different structures and functions.

wrongExplains.0: Polarity here refers to structural orientation, not electrical charge.
wrongExplains.2: Rapid division is regeneration, not polarity.
wrongExplains.3: Polarity means organized orientation, the opposite of random.

scaffolds.q: A characteristic of epithelium is having lots of extracellular matrix. True or false?
options: False, epithelium has minimal ECM and tightly packed cells
True, but only in stratified epithelium
False, epithelium has no cells at all
scaffolds.explain: FALSE — epithelium has MINIMAL ECM and TIGHTLY PACKED cells. Abundant ECM is a characteristic of CONNECTIVE tissue, not epithelial tissue.

wrongExplains.0: Abundant ECM characterizes connective tissue, not epithelium.
wrongExplains.2: Even stratified epithelium has minimal ECM.
wrongExplains.3: Epithelium definitely has cells — they're tightly packed.

scaffolds.q: Epithelial tissues have all of the following characteristics EXCEPT ___?
scaffolds.explain: Epithelium does NOT have large amounts of ECM — that's connective tissue. Epithelial characteristics include TIGHTLY PACKED cells, BASEMENT MEMBRANE, and being AVASCULAR.

wrongExplains.0: Tightly packed cells IS a characteristic of epithelium.
wrongExplains.2: Resting on a basement membrane IS a characteristic of epithelium.
wrongExplains.3: Being avascular IS a characteristic of epithelium.
